witnesses.description: A two-point Sierpinski topology with only trivial clopen subsets.
A two-point discrete topology with an explicit open separation.

checks.claim: The two-point Sierpinski topology is connected because it has only trivial clopen subsets.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates all subsets and checks that only the empty set and universe are clopen.

checks.claim: The two-point discrete topology is disconnected by the open separation {a}, {b}.
checks.notes: The validator checks non-empty disjoint open subsets whose union is the universe.

checks.claim: The subset {a} is a non-trivial clopen subset and therefore witnesses disconnection.
checks.notes: The validator checks that {a} and its complement are open and form a separation.

checks.claim: The two-point discrete topology is connected.

data.certificate: solve_with_drat_proof emits a DRAT refutation for no_nontrivial_clopen = false and no_nontrivial_clopen = true, elaborate_drat_to_lrat emits LRAT, and check_drat/check_lrat independently recheck both proof objects

checks.notes: The validator rejects the connectedness claim by recomputing the non-trivial clopen subset {a}. The DIMACS artifact isolates the false no-nontrivial-clopen claim, and Axeyum emits checked DRAT/LRAT evidence.

checks.claim: General connectedness theorems require future Lean resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: continuous images of connected spaces are connected, intervals in R are connected, and path-connected spaces are connected
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite rows are checked; arbitrary connectedness theorems remain proof-assistant horizons.
